job_stories.title: Write and Test a Prompt
job_stories.situation: I have a prompt idea and want to see how different LLMs respond
job_stories.motivation: quickly test and compare outputs without switching between provider playgrounds
job_stories.outcome: I can evaluate which provider gives the best result for my use case

acceptance_criteria.given: I am on the Prompt Playground page
acceptance_criteria.when: I type a prompt with {{variable}} placeholders in the editor
acceptance_criteria.then: Variable inputs appear below the editor for each detected placeholder

acceptance_criteria.given: I have written a prompt and selected Claude and GPT-4
acceptance_criteria.then: Both providers stream responses simultaneously in side-by-side panels
Each panel shows a live token counter and elapsed time
The panel shows the error message with a 'Retry' button while other panels continue

job_stories.title: Compare Responses Across Providers
job_stories.situation: multiple providers have returned responses to the same prompt
job_stories.motivation: evaluate which response is best for quality, accuracy, and cost
job_stories.outcome: I can make an informed decision about which provider to use

acceptance_criteria.then: Each response shows token count, latency, and estimated cost

acceptance_criteria.when: I click the thumbs up or thumbs down button
acceptance_criteria.then: The rating is saved and visible in the version history for this run

acceptance_criteria.given: I want to read one response in detail
acceptance_criteria.when: I click 'Expand' on a response panel
acceptance_criteria.then: The panel takes full width with the others collapsed to tabs
Markdown is rendered with proper headings, code blocks, and lists

job_stories.title: Version and Iterate on Prompts
job_stories.situation: I have been iterating on a prompt across multiple test runs
job_stories.motivation: see what changes I made and how they affected response quality
job_stories.outcome: I can learn what prompt patterns work best and avoid regressions

acceptance_criteria.given: I have run a prompt test
acceptance_criteria.then: A new version is auto-saved with timestamp, prompt text, and quality scores

acceptance_criteria.given: I am in the version history panel
acceptance_criteria.then: A diff view shows additions in green and deletions in red between the two prompts

acceptance_criteria.given: I am viewing a previous version
acceptance_criteria.then: The prompt editor loads that version's text as the active prompt

acceptance_criteria.given: I have multiple versions with quality scores
acceptance_criteria.then: A sparkline chart shows quality score trend across versions
